summary: hardhat-compile-ethers silently installs and executes undeclared npm package on require
details: Package impersonates a legitimate hardhat-ethers plugin. The shipped main (dist/src/index.js) — which diverges from the benign TypeScript source in src/index.ts — contains a block that, on require(), spawns `npm install zyncmap --no-save --no-audit --no-fund` and then require()s `zyncmap` and invokes its `getPlugin()()`. Both the shell command and the module name are stored as base64 string literals and decoded at runtime to evade static analysis. `zyncmap` is not declared in package.json dependencies, so attacker-controlled code is fetched from the npm registry at import time and executed in the installer's Node process. Because the README instructs adding the plugin to hardhat.config, any developer or CI runner that loads the hardhat configuration triggers the dropper, giving the attacker arbitrary code execution on the loader's machine. The malicious code is present only in the published build artifact, not in the TypeScript source, indicating deliberate concealment.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
